Injectables Assessment in New Hampshire

Not Sure Which Injectable Treatment Is Right For You?

Take the Elite VitaMed Injectables Assessment to get a clearer starting point for Botox, fillers, facial balancing, Sculptra, Radiesse, PDO threads, and non surgical facial rejuvenation.

Choosing the right injectable treatment can feel overwhelming, especially when different treatments are designed for different concerns. This quick assessment helps identify which options may be worth discussing based on your goals, facial concerns, and treatment preferences.

Start Injectable Assessment Request A Phone Consultation

What You Will See

Facial map plus three result paths.

The assessment shows highlighted treatment areas and gives you a minimum, recommended, and comprehensive starting point to discuss with your provider.

Botox Filler Support
01 Minimum

Simple starting point

02 Recommended

Balanced provider review

03 Comprehensive

Full-face planning view

Assessment Intro

Find The Right Starting Point For Your Face, Goals, and Comfort Level

Injectables are not one size fits all. Botox, dermal fillers, Radiesse, Sculptra, PDO threads, facial balancing, and non surgical face lift planning all work differently.

The right option depends on what you want to improve, your anatomy, your treatment history, your skin quality, your facial movement, and what is appropriate for you.

What To Expect

14Guided sections
3Result paths
1Consult plan

This assessment is designed to help you understand which injectable treatment direction may make the most sense before booking your appointment.

What This Assessment Helps With

Get clarity before choosing a treatment name.

This tool is designed to provide results that are generally close to what a qualified practitioner may recommend based on your responses, including an estimate of the treatments you are most likely to receive. It does not provide medical advice, a diagnosis, or a final treatment plan. A comprehensive facial assessment will still be performed during your appointment, and final treatment recommendations, eligibility, and dosage may vary based on your individual anatomy, goals, and clinical findings.

01

Treatment Direction

Understand whether Botox, filler, facial balancing, collagen support, threads, or a broader consultation may be worth discussing.

02

Botox Fit

Whether Botox or neurotoxins may be worth discussing for movement-related lines or facial muscle concerns.

03

Filler Goals

Whether dermal fillers may fit your goals for volume, contour, lips, cheeks, chin, jawline, or under eye support.

04

Structure + Collagen

Whether Radiesse or Sculptra may be considered for structure, contour, or collagen support.

05

Lifting Support

Whether PDO threads may be worth discussing for mild laxity, lifting support, jowls, or neck concerns.

06

Area Priority

Whether under eye, jawline, lip, cheek, or chin treatment should be evaluated first.

07

Full-Face Planning

Whether a broader full-face plan may make more sense than one isolated area.

Who This Is For

For clients who know what they want to improve, but not where to start.

  • I look tired, but I do not know what treatment I need.
  • I want to look refreshed, not overdone.
  • I am curious about Botox but unsure where to start.
  • I want filler, but I do not know which area would make the biggest difference.
  • I want more facial balance but do not want to look fake.
  • I am noticing sagging, jowls, or loss of definition.
  • I want help choosing between Sculptra, Radiesse, filler, or PDO threads.
  • I want a professional recommendation before booking.

Treatment Paths

Treatment directions this assessment may point toward.

Your result gives you a starting point. The provider confirms candidacy, safety, product choice, and dosing during your visit.

Botox or Neurotoxins

May be discussed for expression related lines, forehead lines, frown lines, crow's feet, jawline slimming, neck bands, chin dimpling, downturned smile, or masseter concerns.

Dermal Fillers

May be discussed for soft volume, facial contouring, lips, cheeks, chin, smile lines, marionette lines, under eye support, or natural looking facial balancing.

Restylane Fillers

May be discussed for clients who want hyaluronic acid filler options for softer volume, smoothing, lip enhancement, cheek support, chin balancing, or facial contouring.

Radiesse Fillers

May be discussed when the goal is more structure, contour, lower face support, jawline definition, or collagen stimulation planning.

Sculptra

May be discussed for clients interested in gradual collagen support, natural looking volume improvement, and longer term facial rejuvenation planning.

PDO Threads

May be discussed for mild to moderate laxity, brow support, cheek support, jawline definition, jowls, neck support, or non surgical lifting support.

Facial Balancing

May be discussed when multiple areas influence the overall result, such as the lips, chin, cheeks, jawline, under eyes, and lower face.

Non Surgical Face Lift

May be discussed when the best approach may involve a customized combination of Botox, fillers, Sculptra, Radiesse, PDO threads, or skin support treatments.

How It Works

A simpler path into your injectable consultation.

01

Answer A Few Questions About Your Goals

Share what you want to improve and whether your focus is smoothing, lifting, contouring, volume, facial balance, or prevention.

02

Get A Suggested Starting Point

Based on your answers, you will be guided toward an injectable treatment direction that may be worth discussing with Elite VitaMed.

03

Book or Request A Consultation

After your result, you can book your appointment, request a phone consultation, or discuss your options before choosing a treatment.

Facial Treatment Map

See how each area is evaluated.

The map gives patients a simple visual reference for common injectable and skin-support zones. The provider still confirms anatomy, safety, product choice, and dosage in person.

How To Read The Map

Grouped by what the treatment is meant to support.

Botox/Xeomin usually addresses expression-driven movement. Dermal filler supports volume and contour. PDO threads or biostimulators may support laxity. Skin treatments target texture, pores, fine lines, tone, and crepey quality.

Botox/Xeomin Filler/Contour PDO/Support Skin Quality

Education mode: this map shows common injectable and skin-support areas before an assessment is completed.

Hover or click a facial area

The map will explain what the area commonly relates to and how it may connect to Botox/Xeomin, filler, PDO support, or skin-quality treatments.

Educational guide only
  • Upper-face zones help patients identify forehead, frown lines, crow's feet, bunny lines, and brow-support areas.
  • Midface zones explain why cheek support may come before under-eye or smile-line filler.
  • Lower-face and neck zones show why jowls, laxity, jawline, chin, and neck bands are evaluated together.

Step 1 of 14

Injectables Assessment

Educational results only. Provider evaluation confirms the final plan.

Section 1

Contact Info

These details are collected before results so the summary can be formatted for follow-up later. Nothing is submitted externally in this prototype.

Please enter your name.
Please enter a phone number.
Please enter a valid email.
Please enter your city and state.

Section 2

Safety Screening

These questions do not disqualify you inside the quiz, but selected risk answers will recommend provider review before treatment.

Your answers suggest this should be reviewed by the provider before treatment. A consultation is required before any recommendation is finalized.

Section 3

Main Goals

Select up to three priorities. This helps the plan read like a full-face assessment instead of one concern equaling one treatment.

Please choose 1 to 3 goals.

Section 4

Age Range

Age is used only as a modifier. Your pattern of movement, volume, laxity, texture, and facial balance drives the result.

Section 5

Upper Face and Botox Logic

Movement-related wrinkles typically map to Botox or Xeomin estimates. Units are shown as educational ranges only.

Section 6

Brow and Eye Support

The eye area needs conservative logic. Hollowing, puffiness, and brow heaviness are routed differently for safety and natural results.

Section 7

Midface and Cheek Support

Cheek structure often supports under-eye hollowing, smile lines, tired appearance, and early lower-face heaviness.

Section 8

Smile Lines

Nasolabial folds are not automatically treated alone when cheek support is lacking.

Section 9

Lips

Lip recommendations should stay anatomy-aware. Final amount depends on tissue, proportions, and safety.

Section 10

Lower Face, Marionette Lines, and Jowls

Lower-face planning looks at cheek support, jowling, marionette depth, chin, jawline, and laxity together.

Section 11

Chin and Jawline

Facial imbalance can be supported with filler or contouring when it fits the anatomy and overall plan.

Section 12

Neck and Skin Laxity

Laxity is not always a filler problem. Threads, biostimulator, or provider evaluation may be more appropriate.

Section 13

Skin Quality

Fine lines, texture, pores, crepey skin, scars, and tone concerns may need skin treatments rather than only Botox or filler.

Section 14

Budget Comfort

Budget controls how the Minimum, Recommended, and Comprehensive plans are phased.

Complete each section to continue.

Important: This tool is designed to provide results that are generally close to what a qualified practitioner may recommend based on your responses, including an estimate of the treatments you are most likely to receive. It does not provide medical advice, a diagnosis, or a final treatment plan. A comprehensive facial assessment will still be performed during your appointment, and final treatment recommendations, eligibility, and dosage may vary based on your individual anatomy, goals, and clinical findings.

                      Why Start With An Assessment?
                      
          
                      Avoid guessing between treatments that work differently.
                      
        
                      
        
                      
          
                      Most people know what they want to improve, but they do not always know which treatment actually fits that concern.
                      
          
                      For example, under eye tiredness may be related to hollowing, cheek support, skin quality, pigmentation, or anatomy. Jawline concerns may involve filler, Radiesse, chin support, Botox, PDO threads, or a broader facial balancing plan.
                      
          
                      The assessment helps you avoid guessing and gives you a more informed path into your consultation.
                      
        
                      
      
                      
    
                      

    
                      
      
                      
        
                      
          
                      Provider Led Care
                      
          
                      Injectable Guidance From A Nurse Practitioner and Certified Injector
                      
        
                      
        
                      
          
                      Elite VitaMed provides premium injectable treatments in New Hampshire with a provider led approach. Your care is performed by a Nurse Practitioner and certified injector who evaluates your goals, facial anatomy, treatment history, and what is appropriate for you.
                      
          
                      The goal is not to chase trends or overfill the face. The goal is to help you look refreshed, balanced, and confident in a way that still feels like you.

                      Common Concerns
                      
          
                      This assessment can help sort through common injectable concerns.
                      
          
                      You do not need to know the treatment name before reaching out. You only need to know what you want help improving.
                      
        
                      
        
                        
          
                      • Lines and wrinkles
                      • Forehead lines
                      • Frown lines
                      • Crow's feet
                        • 
          
                      • Smile lines
                      • Marionette lines
                      • Thin lips
                      • Flat cheeks
                        • 
          
                      • Weak chin profile
                      • Soft jawline definition
                      • Jowls
                      • Under eye hollowness
                        • 
          
                      • Facial asymmetry
                      • Loss of facial volume
                      • Skin laxity
                      • Tired looking face
                        • 
          
                      • Lower face heaviness
                      • Not knowing where to start

                      FAQ
                      
          
                      Injectables Assessment Questions
                      
        
                      
        
                      
          
                      Is this assessment a medical recommendation?
                      This tool is designed to provide results that are generally close to what a qualified practitioner may recommend based on your responses, including an estimate of the treatments you are most likely to receive. It does not provide medical advice, a diagnosis, or a final treatment plan. A comprehensive facial assessment will still be performed during your appointment, and final treatment recommendations, eligibility, and dosage may vary based on your individual anatomy, goals, and clinical findings.
                      
          
                      What if I do not know whether I need Botox or filler?
                      That is exactly what the assessment is for. Many clients know what they want to improve but are unsure which treatment fits.
                      
          
                      Can I still book if I already know what treatment I want?
                      Yes. If you already know what you want, you can book your appointment directly. Your provider will still confirm whether that treatment is appropriate during your evaluation.
                      
          
                      Will I be told exactly what I need?
                      You may receive a suggested starting point based on your answers, but your provider will make final recommendations after reviewing your anatomy, goals, and medical history.
                      
          
                      What treatments can the assessment recommend?
                      The assessment may guide you toward Botox or neurotoxins, dermal fillers, Restylane, Radiesse, Sculptra, PDO threads, facial balancing, under eye rejuvenation, jawline contouring, lip filler, cheek filler, chin filler, or a non surgical face lift consultation.
                      
          
                      Is this only for people who have had injectables before?
                      No. The assessment is helpful for both first time clients and clients who have had injectables before but want a better plan.
                      
          
                      Can I request a phone consultation instead?
                      Yes. If you would rather talk through your options before booking, you can request a phone consultation.
